title: Key rotation attestation — KMS/HSM rotation SLA tracker
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate key-wrap hierarchy; KEK/DEK design is out of scope.
evidence: Daily report 'kms_rotation_sla' writes per-key rotation evidence to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 3.6 requires key rotation at end-of-cryptoperiod; the UC evidences compliance per key.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 3.6 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.41.3: Key rotation attestation — KMS/HSM rotation SLA tracker.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.41.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 SC-13 (Cryptographic protection) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.41.3: Key rotation attestation — KMS/HSM rotation SLA tracker.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.41.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted to full: SPL provides comprehensive, standalone evidence for Art.9 with specific data sources and actionable thresholds.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.9 (Protection and prevention) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.41.3: Key rotation attestation — KMS/HSM rotation SLA tracker.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.41.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a key's last rotation is older than the cryptoperiod defined in key_policy.csv, the UC fires with key_id, last_rotation, and cryptoperiod.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Keys within their cryptoperiod and keys marked key_type=archive are NOT flagged.

spl:
index=kms (sourcetype=aws:kms OR sourcetype=azure:keyvault OR sourcetype=gcp:kms) action=rotate
| stats max(_time) as last_rotation by key_id tenant
| lookup key_policy.csv key_id OUTPUT cryptoperiod_days key_type
| where key_type!="archive"
| eval age_days=round((now()-last_rotation)/86400,0)
| where age_days>cryptoperiod_days
| table tenant key_id last_rotation age_days cryptoperiod_days
description: Verifies every encryption key is rotated within its policy cryptoperiod. Overdue keys are highlighted and evidence is written to the compliance index for audit.
value: Gives the cryptographic custodian the ability to defend the 'our keys are rotated' claim with per-key evidence rather than a policy document.
implementation: (1) Ingest KMS audit logs; (2) maintain key_policy.csv; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR to the crypto custodian. Indexes required: `index=kms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:kms`, `sourcetype=azure:keyvault`, `sourcetype=gcp:kms`.
visualization: Table of overdue keys, scatter plot age vs cryptoperiod, single-value '% of keys within cryptoperiod'. Key fields: `age_days`, `cryptoperiod_days`, `key_id`, `last_rotation`, `tenant`.
knownFalsePositives: Keys under a temporary freeze (key_status=frozen) for investigation should be excluded via a status filter.
grandmaExplanation: We check on verifies every encryption key is rotated within its policy cryptoperiod. overdue keys are highlighted and evidence is written to the compliance index for audit. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest KMS audit logs; (2) maintain key_policy.csv; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR to the crypto custodian.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=kms (sourcetype=aws:kms OR sourcetype=azure:keyvault OR sourcetype=gcp:kms) action=rotate
| stats max(_time) as last_rotation by key_id tenant
| lookup key_policy.csv key_id OUTPUT cryptoperiod_days key_type
| where key_type!="archive"
| eval age_days=round((now()-last_rotation)/86400,0)
| where age_days>cryptoperiod_days
| table tenant key_id last_rotation age_days cryptoperiod_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Key rotation attestation — KMS/HSM rotation SLA tracker** — Gives the cryptographic custodian the ability to defend the 'our keys are rotated' claim with per-key evidence rather than a policy document.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: kms; **sourcetype**: aws:kms, azure:keyvault, gcp:kms. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=kms, sourcetype=aws:kms. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **key_id tenant**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where key_type!="archive"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>cryptoperiod_days` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, cryptoperiod_days, key_id, last_rotation, tenant.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of overdue keys, scatter plot age vs cryptoperiod, single-value '% of keys within cryptoperiod'.
